sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me see what changed after clicking on the N object. The image shows that clicking on the first N (leftmost) caused it to turn gray/dark gray and a hollow square appeared. Let me check the segmentation to understand the change.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking N object at (16,14)
# Compare before and after
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = current_frame.segmentation

before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after['nodes']}

print("=== N objects after click ===")
for n in after['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, row={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, col={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

print("\n=== New G/g objects ===")
for n in after['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('G', 'g'):
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, row={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, col={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check what happened to the clicked N (id=14)
if 14 in after_nodes:
    print(f"\nN id=14 still exists: color={after_nodes[14]['color']}, pixels={after_nodes[14]['pixels']}")
else:
    print(f"\nN id=14 gone")

# Check for new w objects (hollow squares)
print("\n=== w objects (potential hollow squares) ===")
w_objs = [n for n in after['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'w' and n['pixels'] == 1]
print(f"Count: {len(w_objs)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
